ddls:
CREATE TABLE "data_processing_tasks" (
  "task_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data processing task */,
  "task_name" TEXT /* Name of the task */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the task */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the task */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the task */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the task (e.g., running, completed, failed) */,
  "pipeline_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data processing pipeline */,
  "pipeline_stage" TEXT /* Stage within the pipeline */,
  "error_message" TEXT /* Error message during the task */,
  "execution_environment" TEXT /* Execution environment (e.g., on-prem, cloud) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("task_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "resource_allocations" (
  "alloc_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each resource allocation */,
  "task_id" INTEGER /* ID of the task for which resources are allocated */,
  "resource_type" TEXT /* Type of resource allocated (e.g., CPU, Memory) */,
  "amount_allocated" REAL /* Amount of the resource allocated */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the resource allocation */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the resource allocation */,
  "pipeline_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data processing pipeline */,
  "pipeline_stage" TEXT /* Stage within the pipeline */,
  "cost" REAL /* Cost associated with the resource allocation */,
  "efficiency_rating" INTEGER /* Efficiency rating of resource usage (0-100) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alloc_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_resource_allocations_pipeline_id FOREIGN KEY ("pipeline_id") REFERENCES data_processing_tasks ("pipeline_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_resource_allocations_task_id FOREIGN KEY ("task_id") REFERENCES data_processing_tasks ("task_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., data engineer, data scientist, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login time of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES roles ("role_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the role */,
  "parent_role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent role (for hierarchical roles) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_roles_parent_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_roles_permissions FOREIGN KEY ("permissions") REFERENCES permissions ("permission_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the permission */,
  "module" TEXT /* Module to which the permission applies */,
  "actions" TEXT /* Specific actions allowed within the module */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the report */,
  "content" TEXT /* Content of the report */,
  "generated_time" TEXT /* Time when the report was generated */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the report */,
  "pipeline_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data processing pipeline */,
  "pipeline_stage" TEXT /* Stage within the pipeline */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., performance, resource, cost) */,
  "summary" TEXT /* Summary of the report content */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_pipeline_id FOREIGN KEY ("pipeline_id") REFERENCES data_processing_tasks ("pipeline_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* ID of the metric that triggered the alert */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the alert */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was generated */,
  "handled" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the alert has been handled */,
  "pipeline_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data processing pipeline */,
  "pipeline_stage" TEXT /* Stage within the pipeline */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., performance, resource, cost) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_pipeline_id FOREIGN KEY ("pipeline_id") REFERENCES data_processing_tasks ("pipeline_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alert_handlers" (
  "handler_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert handling event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who handled the alert */,
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* ID of the alert that was handled */,
  "action_taken" TEXT /* Action taken to handle the alert */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the action was taken */,
  "pipeline_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data processing pipeline */,
  "pipeline_stage" TEXT /* Stage within the pipeline */,
  "resolution_time" REAL /* Time taken to resolve the alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("handler_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alert_handlers_pipeline_id FOREIGN KEY ("pipeline_id") REFERENCES data_processing_tasks ("pipeline_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alert_handlers_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alert_handlers_alert_id FOREIGN KEY ("alert_id") REFERENCES alerts ("alert_id")
)
